Terms & Conditions
PE Fund Accounting Tool — Testing Phase
1. TESTING PHASE DISCLAIMER
This tool ("PE Fund Accounting System") is currently in an active testing and development phase. Features, data, and content may change without prior notice. The creator makes no warranties regarding accuracy, completeness, or fitness for any particular purpose. Use at your own risk.
2. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & COPYRIGHT
All content, questions, explanations, calculations, code, design, and educational material within this tool are the exclusive intellectual property of the creator (Shanmuk Sai Bhuma). This content is protected under applicable copyright and intellectual property laws of India and international conventions.
3. PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES
Users are strictly prohibited from: (a) copying, reproducing, or redistributing any content; (b) reverse-engineering or scraping this tool; (c) sharing access credentials or the tool itself with unauthorised persons; (d) using this tool's content for commercial purposes without written permission; (e) creating derivative works based on this tool's material.
4. DATA PRIVACY
Profile information (name, email, institution) is stored exclusively on your device using browser local storage. No data is transmitted to any server. The creator does not collect, process, or share your personal data.
5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
The creator shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from use of this tool, including but not limited to financial decisions made based on content herein. This tool is for educational purposes only and does not constitute professional financial, legal, or accounting advice.
6. LEGAL JURISDICTION
Any disputes arising out of or in connection with this tool shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of courts in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. These terms shall be governed by the laws of India.
7. ACCEPTANCE
By checking the acceptance box and using this tool, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms & Conditions in their entirety.

Fund Summary
Run Fund Setup first
Fund Setup
Enter fund parameters and LP investor commitments. All changes auto-recalculate every downstream tab instantly.
Fund Parameters Auto-Updates All Tabs
Typical: 10 years
Standard: 2%
Standard: 20%
Standard: 8%
Min recommended: 1%
LP Investors — Capital Commitments Total: ₹0
Investor Name
Total Commitment
LP Investor Summary
# Investor Total Commitment Total Called Uncalled Balance % Called % of Total Fund
Capital Calls
Issue capital call notices to LPs. Capital calls are the mechanism by which a PE fund draws down LP commitments into actual cash. No call = no cash = no investments.
📨 Section A — Capital Call Notices
Issue Capital Call Notices Auto-Updates All Tabs
Step 1 — Issue the Notice: Enter the % of commitment being called, select the LP, set the call date and the due date. This creates the Capital Commitment Receivable entry — recording what is due from each LP.
💰 Section B — Capital Received (LP Payment Entries)
Book Capital Received — When LP Pays
Step 2 — Book the Receipt: When an LP transfers the called amount, select the LP from the dropdown and book the receipt. This posts: Dr Cash / Bank  |  Cr Capital Commitment Receivable — [LP Name]. The receivable is cleared and cash enters the fund.
LP Call Progress — Amount Called vs Remaining
All Capital Call Notices — Summary
Call # LP Name Call Date Due Date Purpose % Called Amount Called
Journal Entries — Capital Call Notices (Receivable Created)
Journal Entries — Capital Received from LPs / GP (Section B)
Investment Lifecycle
Record portfolio investments. A fund can only invest capital it has already raised. Complete Fund Setup → Capital Calls first, then invest below.
Capital Deployment—
0% deployed —
⚠ Total invested exceeds total capital called. Check investment amounts.
Portfolio Investments Auto-Updates All Tabs
Company Cost Fair Value Realised (if exited)
Daily Accrual — Quarterly Fund Auto-posts everywhere
Daily Accrual: Income & expenses accrue daily (Annual ÷ 365). Use +/− to add days one at a time — each day posts immediately to Ledger, TB, Financials & NAV.
Cash Receipt: When cash arrives, book receipt → clears receivable, brings cash in.
Cash Payment: When expense is paid, book payment → clears payable, reduces cash.
Income Lines
DescriptionAnnualDaily RateDaysAccrued Total
Expense Lines
💡 Management Fee: Add a line here named "Management Fee" with the annual amount to record it in the books. The fund setup shows it as info-only and does not post any ledger entry automatically — this prevents double-posting. Record quarterly instalments by setting days accrued, then use Cash Settlement to pay.
DescriptionAnnualDaily RateDaysAccrued Total
Cash Settlement & Variance Adjustment
Enter the actual cash received or paid. If it differs from the accrued amount, a variance adjustment journal entry is posted automatically.
More received than accrued → extra income posted  |  Less received than accrued → income reversal posted
Same logic applies for expenses paid.
Posted Entries
Portfolio Summary
# Company Cost Fair Value Unrealised G/L Realised Proceeds Realised G/L Status
Investment Journal Entries
Subscriptions — New Capital Mid-Fund
Subscriptions processed at the NAV per unit computed in the NAV tab. Complete NAV tab first — NAV auto-populates here.
NAV at Subscription Date
NAV per Unit = — —
New Subscriptions Auto-Updates All Tabs
Investor Name Amount Units Issued (auto)
Subscription Journal Entries
Redemptions — Investor Exits
Redemptions at the NAV per unit from the NAV tab. Complete NAV tab first. Capped at investor's holding.
⚡ Real PE Guard Rails — Applied Automatically
✅ Cannot redeem more than invested — Redemption capped at LP's called capital + any subscriptions
✅ Cannot make NAV go negative — Total redemptions cannot exceed fund NAV
✅ Cost basis auto-calculated — From LP's capital account (called capital ÷ units)
✅ Payout = Units × NAV per unit — Priced at current fair NAV only
LP Redemption Capacity From Fund Setup
Complete Fund Setup and Capital Calls first to see LP details.
NAV at Redemption Date
Auto-filled from NAV tab
NAV per Unit = — —

Distributions — Returns to Investors
Cash distributions from realised proceeds. Cannot exceed total realised exits. Allocated automatically to each LP and GP based on their fund ownership %.

Complete Investments tab first to see available pool.

Dr/Cr balances from the General Ledger. Total Debits must equal Total Credits.

Financial Statements
Income & Expenditure Account and Balance Sheet — generated from all ledger entries.
NAV Statement
Net Asset Value, NAV per unit, and PE performance metrics. Calculated AFTER all accruals, investments, subscriptions, redemptions and distributions.
Correct Order: Everything above → then NAV
✅ Capital Calls→ ✅ Investments→ ✅ Accruals→ ✅ Subscriptions→ ✅ Redemptions→ → NAV ←→ Distributions
NAV Statement
Performance Metrics
Capital Account Statements — Per LP
Individual capital account per LP investor. Shows contributions, subscriptions, redemptions, distributions, P&L allocation, and closing balance.

Real PE style — cumulative compounded hurdle accrues every year, even in loss years. The GP earns zero carry until all accumulated unpaid hurdle is fully settled.

Journal Entries — Waterfall
Double-Entry Journal Entries
Steps We Followed — How to Calculate
A clear reference guide to the exact steps used in both HWM and Waterfall calculators. Use this for your exam, interview, or viva.
Real PE Style — Cumulative Compounded Hurdle
High Water Mark — 5 Steps
⚡ Key Real PE Difference: Hurdle accrues every year — even in loss years. Unpaid hurdle carries forward. GP earns zero carry until all accumulated hurdle is cleared. This is how KKR, Blackstone, Apollo operate.
S1
Gross Profit / Loss
Calculate NAV change. If negative — this is the J-Curve phase. Hurdle still accrues.
Gross Profit = Gross Closing NAV − Beginning NAV
S2
Deduct Management Fee
Always charged on committed capital — in profit years AND loss years. Non-negotiable.
Mgmt Fee = Committed Capital × 2% p.a.
NAV after Fee = Gross NAV − Mgmt Fee
S3
Hurdle Accrual ← THE KEY PE STEP
Hurdle accrues on committed capital every period. If profit is insufficient, the unpaid portion carries forward and compounds. This debt to LP must be fully cleared before any carry.
✅ Profit year: hurdle paid from profit, remainder cleared from debt
❌ Loss year: hurdle NOT paid but STILL ACCRUES → debt grows
❌ Low profit: partial payment → shortfall added to carried debt
Hurdle This Period = Capital × Hurdle% (compounded quarterly if applicable)
New Debt = Prior Debt + Hurdle This Period − Amount Paid
S4
Check ALL 3 Conditions
All 3 must pass for carry to be charged:
✅ Cond 1: NAV after fee > HWM (no double charging)
✅ Cond 2: NAV after fee > Hurdle threshold this period
✅ Cond 3: All accumulated prior hurdle debt = ₹0 (fully cleared)
Carry = max(0, NAV after fee − Carry Threshold) × Carry%
S5
Final NAV & Update HWM
Deduct carry to get Final NAV. Update HWM if new all-time high. Carry forward any unpaid hurdle.
Final NAV = NAV after Fee − Performance Fee
New HWM = max(Previous HWM, Final NAV)
Carried Debt → next period
Method 2
Waterfall Provision — 4 Steps
S1
Return of Capital
LP gets their invested capital back first. GP receives nothing here. Remaining = Total Profit available.
Remaining Profit = Exit Value − LP Capital
S2
Preferred Return (Hurdle Rate)
LP receives guaranteed minimum return before GP earns any performance fee. Uses simple interest by default.
Preferred Return = Capital × Hurdle% × Years
(Compound: Capital × [(1+r)^n − 1])
S3
GP Catch-Up
GP receives profit until their total share = Carry% of Total Profit. Since GP received ₹0, they need the full target.
GP Target = Total Profit × Carry%
GP Catch-Up = min(GP Target, Remaining Pool)
S4
Profit Split
Remaining profit split between LP and GP at the agreed carried interest ratio.
LP Share = Remaining × (1 − Carry%)
GP Share = Remaining × Carry%
Distribution Flow
1
Return Capital → LP gets back investment
↓
2
Preferred Return → LP gets hurdle rate
↓
3
GP Catch-Up → GP reaches target share
↓
4
Profit Split → LP (80%) + GP (20%)
HWM vs Waterfall — Quick Comparison
Feature HWM — Real PE Style Waterfall Method
Purpose Annual NAV tracking, ongoing performance fees One-time exit distribution of total proceeds
When Used Every year during 10-year fund life At fund exit / portfolio realisation
Hurdle Treatment CUMULATIVE — accrues every year, even loss years. Carries forward. Paid at exit from total proceeds (Tier 2)
Carry Trigger 3 conditions: NAV > HWM AND > Hurdle AND all prior debt cleared After capital + pref return + catch-up are satisfied
Loss Year Effect Hurdle still accrues — adds to LP debt. GP earns ₹0 carry. Not applicable (single exit event)
J-Curve Years 1–3 typically loss years. Hurdle debt builds up. Reflected in lower exit value / MoI
Mgmt Fee Deducted every period (2% p.a.) — no exceptions Paid separately, not part of waterfall tiers
HWM Role Prevents double-charging on NAV recovery after drawdown Not applicable
Used by KKR, Blackstone, Apollo, all institutional PE funds Standard PE exit waterfall globally
12 Rules You Must Know — Real PE Style
1 Mgmt fee is ALWAYS charged
Even in a loss year — 2% p.a. on committed capital is deducted every single period, no exceptions.
2 Hurdle accrues in loss years too ⚡
This is the key real PE rule. The fund does NOT pay hurdle from capital. It accrues as a debt and carries forward.
3 Unpaid hurdle compounds forward ⚡
Shortfall adds to "Preferred Return Payable" balance. GP cannot earn a single rupee of carry until this is zero.
4 All 3 conditions must pass for carry
(1) NAV > HWM, (2) NAV > Hurdle threshold, (3) All accumulated hurdle debt = ₹0 fully cleared.
5 HWM only moves upward
Once set, HWM never decreases. Only updates when Final NAV (after all fees) sets a new all-time high.
6 J-Curve is a real phenomenon
PE funds lose money in early years (fees + slow deployment). Normal — hurdle debt builds and clears when exits happen.
7 Quarterly: use compounded rate
Annual 8% → quarterly rate = (1.08)^(1/4) − 1 = 1.94% per quarter. Not simply 8÷4=2%.
8 Waterfall Step 1 is always LP capital
LP always recovers full invested capital before any profit is shared. GP gets nothing in Tier 1.
9 GP Catch-Up = Total Profit × Carry%
In waterfall, GP target = Total Profit × 20%. Not based on pref return ratio. They receive nothing until this target is met.
10 Grand Total must equal Exit Value
In waterfall: LP Total + GP Total = Exit Value. If not, there is a calculation error somewhere.
11 ILPA standard is cumulative
Institutional Limited Partners Association recommends cumulative compounded preferred return for all PE funds globally.
12 Journal: Dr = Cr always
Every journal entry must balance. Hurdle accrual is Dr Preferred Return Expense / Cr Preferred Return Payable — even if not paid in cash.
Key PE Concepts & Accounting
Complete reference for definitions, formulae, and journal entries.
High Water Mark

The highest-ever Final NAV after all fees. Protects LPs from paying twice when NAV recovers from a loss.

New HWM = max(Prev HWM, Final NAV)

Performance Fee Conditions
Both must be satisfied:
✅ NAV after mgmt fee > HWM
✅ NAV after mgmt fee > Hurdle Level
Preferred Return

Minimum guaranteed return (8% p.a.) LP must receive before GP earns any carry. Paid before catch-up and profit split.

Simple: Capital × Rate × Years
Compound: Capital × [(1+r)^n−1]

GP Catch-Up

GP receives profits until their total share = Carry% of Total Profit. Ensures GP catches up before the residual split.

GP Target = Total Profit × Carry%
Catch-Up = min(Target, Remaining Pool)

Management Fee

Charged every year regardless of performance. Typically 2% of committed capital. Deducted before performance fee is calculated.

Quarterly = Annual Rate ÷ 4

MoI & IRR

MoI = Exit ÷ Capital (2× = doubled money)

IRR = annualised return. If IRR > Hurdle, GP earns carry.

IRR ≈ (Exit ÷ Capital)^(1/n) − 1

Complete Journal Entry Reference
Transaction Debit Credit
LP Capital Contribution Cash / Bank A/c LP Capital A/c
Investment Purchase Investment Portfolio A/c Cash / Bank A/c
Unrealised Gain Investment Portfolio A/c Unrealised Gain — OCI A/c
Unrealised Loss Unrealised Loss — P&L A/c Investment Portfolio A/c
Management Fee Accrual Management Fee Expense A/c Mgmt Fee Payable A/c (GP)
Management Fee Payment Mgmt Fee Payable A/c (GP) Cash / Bank A/c
Performance Fee Accrual Performance Fee Expense A/c Perf Fee Payable A/c (GP)
Performance Fee Payment Perf Fee Payable A/c (GP) Cash / Bank A/c
Distribution — Return of Capital LP Capital A/c Cash / Bank A/c
Distribution — Preferred Return Preferred Return Payable A/c Cash / Bank A/c
GP Catch-Up Distribution Perf Fee Payable A/c (GP) Cash / Bank A/c
Clawback (GP repays) Cash / Bank A/c LP Capital A/c

Admin Control Centre
PE Fund Accounting System — Master Feature Manager
🟢 FREE USER — What They Can Access
• All tabs/features marked FREE
• MCQ Levels 1–5 by default (you can change below)
• Free Interview Questions (30 Qs)
• All items with toggle = ON + tier = FREE
💎 PRO USER — What They Can Access
• Everything FREE users can access, PLUS:
• All tabs/features marked PRO
• MCQ Levels 6–15 by default (you can change below)
• Pro Interview Questions (30 Qs)
• Pro unlocked via password: ••••••••
📖 How to use this panel:   Each item has a Toggle (ON/OFF) and a Tier (FREE / PRO) button.
— Toggle OFF = completely hidden from ALL users (they won't even see it exists).
— Toggle ON + Tier FREE = visible and usable by everyone, no password needed.
— Toggle ON + Tier PRO = shown with a 🔒 lock to free users; pro users can access it after entering the pro password.
After making changes, click 💾 Save & Apply to activate them immediately.
📑 Tab Visibility Control Disabled tabs show 🔒 Upgrade to Pro in Free mode
How this works:
🟢 FREE = All users can see and use this tab.
💎 PRO = Only Pro users can access. Free users see a 🔒 lock badge on the tab.
🔴 Toggle OFF = Tab is completely hidden from the navigation for everyone.

Changes take effect immediately when you click FREE/PRO or the toggle switch.
🎯 MCQ Level Access Control — 15 Quiz Levels
How this works — very simple:
🟢 FREE = Anyone can play this level, no password needed.
💎 PRO = Only Pro users (who entered the pro password) can play. Free users see a 🔒 lock.
🚫 Toggle OFF = Level is completely hidden from everyone.

To change a level: Click 🟢 FREE or 💎 PRO button under that level. Changes apply instantly — no save button needed.
📋 Interview Questions Access Control
ℹ️  Control access to Free and Pro interview question tiers. You can disable specific difficulty levels (Easy / Medium / Advanced) within each tier. Disabled sections show a lock badge with "Upgrade to Pro" message.

⚠️ Danger Zone — Destructive Actions
⚠️ These actions cannot be undone. Proceed with extreme caution.
Reset Admin Settings to Default
Re-enables all tabs, clears all feature overrides. Does NOT wipe fund data.
Reset Admin Password to Default
Reverts admin password to ADMIN@FUND2025. Use if locked out.
Clear All Pro User Access
Removes stored pro unlock flag from this browser session.
🗑️ Wipe All Fund Data
Clears all fund setup, transactions, journal entries. Irreversible!
